sn36.4:1.1: “Monks, when an uninstructed run-of-the-mill person makes the statement,
sn36.4:1.2: ‘There is a bottomless chasm in the ocean,’
sn36.4:1.3: he is talking about something that doesn’t exist, that can’t be found.
sn36.4:1.5: The word ‘bottomless chasm’ is actually a designation for painful bodily feeling.
sn36.4:1.6: “When an uninstructed run-of-the-mill person is touched by a painful bodily feeling, he sorrows, grieves, & laments, beats his breast, becomes distraught.
sn36.4:1.7: This is called an uninstructed run-of-the-mill person who has not risen up out of the bottomless chasm, who has not gained a foothold.
sn36.4:1.8: “When a well-instructed disciple of the noble ones is touched by a painful bodily feeling, he does not sorrow, grieve, or lament, does not beat his breast or become distraught.
sn36.4:1.9: This is called a well-instructed disciple of the noble ones who has risen up out of the bottomless chasm, whose foothold is gained.”
sn36.4:3.3: He hasn’t risen up out of the bottomless chasm
sn36.4:4.4: who doesn’t tremble at their touch:
sn36.4:4.5: He’s risen up out of the bottomless chasm;
